content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 11, 1989


Bruce T. Olson
Executive Director
American Grand Jury Foundation
1015 12th Street, Suite 6
Modesto, CA  95354-0811

Re:  Your Request for Advice
     Our File No. I-89-402

Dear Mr. Olson:

     You have requested information regarding the financial
interest disclosure requirements for grand jurors.  Enclosed is a
copy of a letter to a presiding judge explaining the Commission's
determination that county conflicts of interest codes are required
to provide for financial disclosure by grand jurors, and
suggesting the types of economic interests which grand jurors
should disclose.  Also enclosed is a copy of the Form 730
(Statement of Economic Interests for Designated Employees) which
grand jurors and other officials and employees of public agencies
use to disclose their economic interests.

     While we have not prepared materials specifically for grand
jurors, our staff is preparing a seminar around the state in
which will deal among other things with adopting conflict of
interest codes for grand jurors.  Enclosed is information
regarding those seminars.

     If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5901.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel

                                        By: John G. McLean
                                            Counsel, Legal Division

KED/JGM/aa

Enclosures


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

FPPC

JUL 3 3 23 PM '09

June 29, 1989


Mr. John H. Larson, Chair
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Larson:

I would appreciate receiving a copy of the most current information
available regarding the Statement of Economic Interest form requirements
for California grand jurors.

It would be most helpful if I could have this information in time for
the Tenth Annual Grand Jury Seminar, August 17-19, 1989. I am enclosing
a copy of our most recent newsletter in which more information about the
Seminar is provided.

Sincerely,

Bruce T. Olson, Ph.D.
Executive Director

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 6, 1989


Bruce T. Olson, Ph.D.
Executive Director
American Grand Jury Foundation
1015 - 12th Street, Suite 6
Modesto, CA  95354-0811

Letter No. 89-402

Dear Dr. Olson:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act
was received on July 3, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices
Commission. If you have any questions about your advice request,
you may contact John McLean, an attorney in the Legal Division,
directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. If more
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to
information needed. If your request is for informal assistance,
we will answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,


Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

sections.question_synthetic: The American Grand Jury Foundation requested information about the financial interest disclosure requirements and procedures that apply to grand jurors in California.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advised that county conflict of interest codes are required to provide for financial disclosure by grand jurors. The Commission provided a Form 730 (Statement of Economic Interests) for use by grand jurors and offered information about seminars on adopting conflict of interest codes for grand jurors.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of three parts: (1) formal FPPC advice response dated September 11, 1989; (2) incoming request letter dated June 29, 1989; and (3) August 1989 newsletter. No explicit QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections with headers, but question and answer are clearly embedded in correspondence. July 6 receipt letter also included.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: The American Grand Jury Foundation requested information about the financial interest disclosure requirements and procedures that apply to grand jurors in California.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC advised that county conflict of interest codes are required to provide for financial disclosure by grand jurors. The Commission provided a Form 730 (Statement of Economic Interests) for use by grand jurors and offered information about seminars on adopting conflict of interest codes for grand jurors.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter file 89-402 responding to the American Grand Jury Foundation's request for information about Statement of Economic Interest form requirements and disclosure obligations for California grand jurors. The FPPC provided relevant materials, referenced Form 730, and informed about upcoming seminars.
